title: Protocol for Abscisic Acid (ABA) Extraction from Plant Seeds
author: Jian You Wang
materials: Weight 100 mg fine powder of ground seeds.
Tap the bottle to remove any bubbles.
ABA quantification is performed by LC-MS/MS using a UHPLC-Triple-Stage Quadrupole Mass Spectrometer (Thermo Scientific Altis) machine.

steps.title: Grind 7-8 seeds in a Safe-Lock 2
steps.instruction: Grind 7-8 seeds in a Safe-Lock 2.0 mL Eppendorf tube with 3-4 beads for 00:01:00 – frequency 25-26 Hz.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Standard solution in each tube
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Standard solution in each tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 14000 rpm, , 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 14000 rpm, , 5 minutes , then transfer the supernatant to a new 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Add another 1 mL of Standard solution
steps.instruction: Add another 1 mL of Standard solution.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant ( 2 mL...
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant ( 2 mL ) to a 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Dry the supernatant under vacuum by speedVac for 02:00:00
steps.instruction: Dry the supernatant under vacuum by speedVac for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: After evaporating the solvent, either keep...
steps.instruction: After evaporating the solvent, either keep the extracted samples at -20 °C or proceed to the next step.

steps.title: Re-dissolve the final extract in 120 µL of acetonitrile
steps.instruction: Re-dissolve the final extract in 120 µL of acetonitrile : water [25:75 (v/v)] followed by 00:01:00 sonication.

steps.title: Filter the re-suspended solution through a 0
steps.instruction: Filter the re-suspended solution through a 0.22 µm filter into 0.1 mL micro-insert 29x5.7 mm clear glass tubes with inserted vials.

steps.title: Chromatographic separation is achieved on the...
steps.instruction: Chromatographic separation is achieved on the Hypersil GOLD C18 Selectivity HPLC Columns (150 × 4.6 mm; 3 μm; fisher scientific) with mobile phases consisting of water (A) and acetonitrile (B), both containing 0.1% formic acid, and the following linear gradient (flow rate, 0.5 mL/min): 0–10 min, 15%–100 % B, follow it by washing with 100 % B for 00:05:00 and equilibration with 15 % B for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Inject 10 µL of sample, maintain...
steps.instruction: Inject 10 µL of sample, maintain the column temperature at 35 °C for each run.

steps.title: Set the MS parameters of Thermo...
steps.instruction: Set the MS parameters of Thermo ScientificTM AltisTM as follows: negative mode, ion source of H-ESI, ion spray voltage of 3000 V, sheath gas of 40 arbitrary units, aux gas of 15 arbitrary units, sweep gas of 0 arbitrary units, ion transfer tube gas temperature of 350 °C , vaporizer temperature of 350 °C , collision energy of 20 eV, CID gas of 2 mTorr, and full width at half maximum (FWHM) 0.4 Da of Q1/Q3 mass. The characteristic Multiple Reaction Monitoring (MRM) transitions (precursor ion → product ion) were the characteristic MRM transitions (precursor ion → product ion) were 263.2 → 153.1 for ABA; 269.2 → 159.1 for D6-ABA.
